title: Typed Tool Contracts, JSON Schema, Descriptions, Domains, and Versions · Program Brief: Tool Descriptions
summary: A broad execute tool accepts free-form strings, hides side effects, uses ambiguous parameter names, has no bounds or schema version, and exposes more authority than the task needs. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tool descriptions increment in least-capability typed tool catalog. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.
Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-tool-schema-design-4
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-tool-schema-design-4.
# Case 1655: a controlled support-operations rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def worked_agentpy_tool_schema_design_4(tool_schemas: int = 8, valid_schemas: int = 8, ambiguous_parameters: int = 0, bounded_enums: int = 6, schema_versions: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorityworked4",
        "interaction1655",
        "tool4dekrow",
        "stateworked41655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if tool_schemas < 3 or valid_schemas < 0:
        return False, valid_schemas, "tool-schema-count"
    if valid_schemas != tool_schemas:
        return False, valid_schemas, "tool-schema-invalid"
    if ambiguous_parameters > 0 or bounded_enums < 2:
        return False, ambiguous_parameters, "parameter-ambiguity"
    if schema_versions < 2:
        return False, schema_versions, "tool-schema-version"
    assert valid_schemas == tool_schemas and ambiguous_parameters == 0
    return True, valid_schemas, "typed-tool-catalog"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Tool Descriptions model · Theory Tool Descriptions

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.
